seo.title: AG2 Guardrails for Government
seo.description: Compliance guide for AG2 agents in government. Meet FedRAMP, FISMA requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Government teams using AG2 for multi-agent conversations face heightened risks due to the framework's group chat dynamics and code execution capabilities, which can lead to off-topic spirals, factual errors, and harmful outputs in sensitive environments. Compliance with FedRAMP, FISMA, and NIST 800-53 is mandatory, requiring robust guardrails to prevent national security breaches and ensure explainable AI decisions. Vex provides essential message hooks to enforce these regulations at runtime.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires continuous monitoring, audit logging, and protection of controlled unclassified information (CUI) in cloud-hosted AI systems.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex message hooks capture all AG2 conversations for FedRAMP-compliant audit trails and block CUI exfiltration attempts.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates vulnerability scanning and real-time monitoring of AI systems for malicious behavior.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans AG2 agent messages for common failure modes like off-topic drift and harmful code generation before execution.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires algorithmic impact assessments and risk management for high-impact AI systems affecting citizens.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex provides runtime compliance scoring and explainability reports for AG2 group chat decisions.

complianceRequirements.description: All AI-assisted decisions must be explainable, auditable, and appealable to citizens.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex generates decision provenance logs tracing AG2 speaker selection and consensus processes.

industryRisks.name: Classified Data Exfiltration
industryRisks.description: AG2 agents inadvertently leaking classified information through external LLM calls or code execution.
industryRisks.example: Group chat references TOP SECRET//NOFORN data, triggering external API call to commercial LLM.

industryRisks.name: Decision Bias Amplification
industryRisks.description: Speaker selection bias in AG2 group chats leading to discriminatory outcomes in citizen services.
industryRisks.example: Benefits eligibility agent consistently defers to biased speaker, denying valid claims.

industryRisks.name: Harmful Code Generation
industryRisks.description: Code execution agents producing malware or disruptive scripts during policy analysis.
industryRisks.example: Security policy simulation agent generates ransomware payload as 'threat model'.

industryRisks.name: Factual Consensus Failure
industryRisks.description: Group chat reaches incorrect consensus on critical facts, affecting national security decisions.
industryRisks.example: Intelligence analysis agents agree on fabricated threat actor attribution.

caseStudyScenario.heading: AG2 in Government: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: The Department of Defense Policy Analysis Team deployed AG2 to simulate national security scenarios across six specialized agents: Intelligence Analyst, Legal Advisor, Cyber Operations, Budget Analyst, Political Advisor, and Decision Coordinator. The group chat was tasked with evaluating cyber policy responses to a foreign adversary's election interference campaign. Without guardrails, the conversation rapidly spiraled when the Political Advisor introduced unrelated domestic surveillance topics, while the Cyber Operations agent began generating network scanning code 'for threat modeling.' The Intelligence Analyst referenced classified SIGINT data, triggering an external LLM call that risked exfiltration. Speaker selection bias emerged as the Budget Analyst dominated consensus, prioritizing cost over security.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex message hooks immediately intervened: classified data patterns were blocked with provenance logging, conversation drift was corrected with topic reinforcement, and the malicious code was sandboxed before execution. The Decision Coordinator received an explainability report documenting speaker contributions (Budget Analyst: 42%, Legal: 28%) and compliance score (94/100). The session completed with auditable, explainable recommendations that passed FISMA review. Post-deployment audit revealed Vex blocked 17 compliance violations across 42 sessions, preventing potential congressional investigation.

cta.heading: Comply with Government Regulations Using Vex
